prompt: Count customers whose total gross sales exceed the average gross sales per customer in the current filter context. Grounded in Retail-v2.

assertions.text: Iterates the customer dimension with VALUES('Customer'[Customer ID]) (or the customer table), not the fact rows
Relies on context transition to get each customer's own total — references [Gross Sales] (or CALCULATE) inside the iterator so the measure re-evaluates per customer
Computes the threshold with AVERAGEX over customers (average of per-customer totals), NOT a flat AVERAGE of fact rows or AVERAGE of a column
Freezes the average in a VAR before counting, so it is not recomputed per row / under the wrong context
Counts customers above the threshold via COUNTROWS(FILTER(... [Gross Sales] > _avg ...)) or equivalent
